title: Gammon with Pineapple
subtitle: Pan-fried gammon steak with caramelised pineapple rings and a fried egg
excerpt: Gammon steak with pineapple is a British retro classic: salty cured pork fried in butter alongside rings of pineapple until the edges caramelise, served with a fried egg. It is ready in 15 minutes and needs nothing more complicated than a hot pan.
sourceNotes: British pub and domestic cooking from the 1960s onwards. The combination of gammon and pineapple reached peak popularity in British pub menus in the 1970s and 1980s as tinned pineapple became a pantry staple. Remains a nostalgic comfort dish.

content.text: cooks quickly and well in a hot pan. The only technique that matters is scoring the fat edge before it goes in: without scoring, the fat shrinks faster than the meat and the steak curls up into a dish shape, cooking unevenly.

should go in the same pan after the gammon is done, using the rendered fat to caramelise the rings. A tinned pineapple ring is perfectly correct here and slightly better than fresh for this dish because it is already soft.

items.name: Gammon steak
items.prepNote: approx. 200 g each, fat edge scored at 2 cm intervals

items.name: Pineapple
items.prepNote: cut into rings approx. 1 cm thick, or use 4 tinned rings in juice, drained

content.text: in a large frying pan over high heat until foaming. Add the
(no salt: the cure provides enough). Fry for 3-4 minutes per side until golden with dark edges and cooked through. Remove to a warm plate and rest for 2 minutes.

In the same pan with the rendered fat, add the pineapple rings over high heat. Cook for 2 minutes per side until golden and caramelised at the edges. If frying eggs, do so now in the same pan. Plate the gammon, top with pineapple rings, and add the fried egg if using.

rows.fix: Score the fat at 2 cm intervals before the steak goes in. The cuts allow the fat to expand without pulling the meat into a curve.
rows.cause: Fat edge was not scored before cooking
rows.symptom: Gammon steak curls up in the pan

rows.fix: Soak the raw steaks in cold water for 20-30 minutes before cooking and pat dry.
rows.cause: The cure level varies between brands; some are heavily brined

rows.fix: Drain tinned rings thoroughly on kitchen paper. Fresh rings must be patted dry. Cook in a very hot pan and do not move them for the first 2 minutes.
rows.cause: Excess moisture in the pan, or tinned rings not properly drained
rows.symptom: Pineapple rings are watery rather than caramelised

rows.fix: Cook to an internal temperature of 71°C. Thick steaks take 4-5 minutes per side; thinner steaks 2-3 minutes.
rows.cause: Overcooked; gammon dries out quickly beyond the right internal temperature

content.text: Gammon and pineapple is a dish that provokes strong opinions: it is loved as a nostalgic comfort and dismissed as dated. It has been on the menu at British pubs almost continuously since the 1970s and shows no sign of disappearing. At home, it is a 15-minute supper that requires one pan and produces something satisfying. Serve with chips and a fried egg.
